title: SDK With Open Graph Provider
description: Graph operations require an opened `CodeGraphProvider`, but hosts should not reimplement open/close/error cleanup in every command. This spec defines a shared lifecycle helper that opens a provider from an `SdkHostContext`, runs a callback, and closes on success or failure.
context: Graph operations require an opened `CodeGraphProvider`, but hosts should not reimplement open/close/error cleanup in every command. This spec defines a shared lifecycle helper that opens a provider from an `SdkHostContext`, runs a callback, and closes on success or failure.

rules:
`withOpenGraphProvider<T>(ctx: SdkHostContext, fn: (provider: CodeGraphProvider) => Promise<T>): Promise<T>` SHALL:

1. Call `ctx.createGraphProvider()` to obtain a provider
2. Call `provider.open()`
3. Invoke `fn(provider)` and return its result
4. Call `provider.close()` in a `finally` block after `fn` completes or throws

When `fn` throws, `withOpenGraphProvider` MUST still attempt `provider.close()`. Close failures during error cleanup MUST NOT mask the original error from `fn`. The original error MUST propagate to the caller.
Unlike CLI-specific `withProvider`, the SDK helper MUST NOT call `process.exit()`. Signal handling and forced exit semantics remain the responsibility of the host adapter (CLI change 12).

`withOpenGraphProvider` MAY accept an optional `beforeOpen` callback invoked after provider creation and before `open()`, for host-specific setup (e.g. lock acquisition in CLI). When omitted, the flow proceeds directly to `open()`.

scenarios.name: Provider opened and closed around callback
then: AND provider.close() is called after fn completes

scenarios.name: Original error preserved when close fails during cleanup
given: AND provider.close() also throws during cleanup
then: error E propagates to the caller

scenarios.name: SDK helper does not exit process
then: process.exit is not called by the SDK implementation

scenarios.name: beforeOpen runs before open
then: beforeOpen(provider) runs after provider creation and before open()
